paper_title: A Verifiable Search Is Not a Learnable Chain-of-Thought
author: Harsh Patel

claims.statement: Solver coverage does not predict whether a verifiable search distills into a chain-of-thought.

evidence.description: Three-way plot: solver (backtracking search) vs. forward-derivable ceiling vs. fine-tuned model. For cryptarithm, solver reaches 71% but forward-derivable ceiling collapses to ~0% and model tracks that, not the solver.
Per-task comparison of solver accuracy vs. model accuracy. Gap is near-zero for four tasks (solver ≥98%, model ≥0.99) and exceeds 0.65 for cryptarithm (solver 71%, model 0.01-0.07).

claims.evidence_quote: Solver coverage, in short, predicts model accuracy only when the solver is a forward map. For search procedures it predicts almost nothing.

claims.statement: A search over information-free structure admits no faithful forward chain-of-thought to imitate.

evidence.description: Forward derivation coverage: a closure applying only steps the base provably executes (column arithmetic, injectivity) covers only 1/659 instances.

MI(digit;glyph) = 0.021 nats vs. shuffle baseline of 0.005 nats, gap strata recover 17% of rows. Structural predictors all score at or below analytic chance rate of 1/10 on leave-one-digit-out prediction.

claims.evidence_quote: Because the cipher is information-free, no single column reveals a digit: the map is pinned only by requiring all example equations to hold simultaneously...

claims.statement: Cryptarithm-deduce accuracy never exceeds 0.07 across eleven successive CoT designs, RLVR/GRPO, and STaR.

evidence.description: One floor, many rounds: cryptarithm-deduce accuracy under successive CoT designs. Nine reaching a held-out greedy eval are shown; all land at 0.01-0.07.
14 cryptarithm training runs (various designs: boot, twn, honest, grpo). Every run's held-out deduce accuracy stays in 0.01-0.07 range.

claims.evidence_quote: Every round lands in 0.01-0.07. The truncation rate swings wildly across rounds, from 50% down to 14%, while accuracy does not move, which already rules out 'out of budget' as the explanation.

claims.statement: Under SFT, arithmetic is correct on 97-100% of lines but elimination verdicts are emitted as unconditional templates (fidelity 16-57%).

evidence.description: Per-line fidelity audit across cryptarithm elimination-line types. Arithmetic on the line is essentially perfect while the line's verdict ('therefore drop this candidate') follows from its own numbers only 16-57% of the time.

659 transcript line records scored on three axes: transcription, arithmetic, and verdict. The dominant elimination type ('enumerate ones-residues, no matches, drop this candidate') has arithmetic correctness ~99% yet verdict correctness only ~27%.

claims.evidence_quote: The model has learned the form of a verifiable elimination step as a fixed template, decoupled from the logic the step is supposed to encode.

claims.statement: The search-distillation ceiling holds across four backbones (3B to 21B, hybrid Mamba/MoE, dense Transformer, MoE Transformer).

evidence.description: Four backbones fine-tuned with rank-32 LoRA on identical cryptarithm corpus: Nemotron-Nano-30B (3.5B act) ≤0.04, Llama 3.2 3B ≤0.04, Qwen 2.5 3B ≤0.04, GPT-OSS 5B ≤0.04.
DeepSeek-V3 (671B) in-context: 0.03 (finishes, wrong). Nemotron-Super-49B in-context: 0.01 (79% truncate).

claims.evidence_quote: The search-distillation failure is therefore architecture-general, and the binding constraint is task-intrinsic (no faithful forward CoT to imitate), not the SSM backbone.

claims.statement: Forward-derivability is causal: revealing the full cipher key lifts cryptarithm-deduce from 0.03 to 0.571; revealing only half barely helps.

evidence.description: Same cryptarithm instances with fraction of cipher key revealed: none → 0.03 (CI [0.01, 0.08]), half → 0.05 (CI [0.02, 0.13]), full → 0.571 (CI [0.45, 0.68]).

claims.evidence_quote: Two things follow. First, forward-derivability is causal: it is the lever, holding the surface task fixed. Second, the effect is sharply nonlinear: half-forward does not give half-accuracy, because any residual search re-introduces the verdict-as-token failure.

claims.statement: The binding bottleneck is derivation, not ranking: the model can recognize a correct cipher (hit@8=0.71) but cannot construct one forward (1/659).

evidence.description: With budget-unbounded generate-and-verify: hit@8 = 0.71 (gold answer in model's top 8 on 71% of instances). When requiring forward arithmetic closure instead: coverage collapses to 1/659.

claims.evidence_quote: The model can recognize a correct map but cannot construct one left-to-right, because constructing it requires tracking which assignments are still consistent, explicit search state, across the whole problem.

claims.statement: Training loss is not a signal for this kind of learnability: all 14 cryptarithm SFT rounds drove train NLL to the floor while held-out accuracy stayed ≤0.07.

evidence.description: Kaggle merge runs logged final loss ~1.22. Tinker warm-start reached NLL 0.04. Cryptarithm rounds reached NLL 0.013 by step 200 and floor by second epoch. Held-out deduce accuracy never left 0.01-0.07 across all 14 rounds.

claims.evidence_quote: Train loss is not a signal for this kind of learnability; only held-out verification is.

claims.statement: The search-distillation ceiling is not a ceiling on the task: memorizing the search's finite structure reaches Private LB 0.92.

evidence.description: Team NullSira's solution: 4,205-entry per-signature candidate catalog for cryptarithm, 5,238-entry rule-sequence catalog for bit-manipulation. Precomputed offline, memorized through SFT, verified in-trace. Reaches Private LB 0.92.

claims.evidence_quote: What transfers is therefore not search but memorization and verification. This sharpens the boundary: a verifiable search is learnable as a chain-of-thought exactly when its candidate structure compresses to a catalog small enough to memorize and a residual cheap enough to verify in-budget.

claims.statement: Non-native CoT voice causes 0% greedy adoption despite 1.0 teacher-forced fidelity: extreme exposure bias at the very first token.

evidence.description: Grammars authored in non-native voice (opening with 'Bit...' or 'Group...' rather than 'I...'): teacher-forced fidelity was 1.0, the model demonstrably learned them, yet at greedy decoding it entered them 0% of the time.

claims.evidence_quote: The grammar is a fully-learned but unreachable island. Cryptarithm, whose traces happen to share the native opener, does enter the grammar and then drifts mid-trace.
